REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT GLORIA THOMAS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO SET ASIDE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IS CONTINUED TO JANUARY 19, 2023. THE COURT IS INCLINED TO SET THIS SETTLEMENT ASIDE. HOWEVER, THE COURT WILL GIVE PLAINTIFF AN OPPORTUNITY TO FILE A MOTION TO ENFORCE THE SETTLEMENT AND TO ENTER A JUDGMENT PER THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT AS IT APPEARS FROM THE OPPOSITION THAT PLAINTIFF BELIEVES THAT THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IS ENFORCEABLE. PLAINTIFF SHALL FILE AND PROPERLY NOTICE MOTION TO ENFORCE TO BE HEARD ON JANUARY 19, 2023. THE COURT NOTES THAT THE MOVING PARTY FAILED TO COOPERATE WITH THE PROBATE PROCEEDINGS AND FAILED TO ADDRESS THIS FAILURE IN THE REPLY. THIS PERSISTENT FAILURE CONTRADICTS MOVING PARTY'S CLAIM THAT SHE "REMAINS INTERESTED IN AN INFORMAL RESOLUTION OF THIS SUIT AND WILL EARNESTLY ATTEMPT ONE AS SOON AS ALL PROPER PERSONS ARE PARTIES TO THE SUIT." JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Notice Of Motion, And Motion, To Set Aside Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Jan-19-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT GLORIA THOMAS' NOTICE OF MOTION, AND MOTION, TO SET ASIDE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT is GRANTED. The settlement agreement is set aside. The action is placed back on the case management calendar and stayed as to all aspects of the case until all necessary parties are joined in this action, i.e. Theodore's successors-in-interest. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-08-2022 Continued To Mar-07-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion, And Motion, To Set Aside Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Nov-18-2022 Continued To Jan-19-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Eli Underwood; Underwood Law Firm; 916-318-8000; appearing on behalf of defendant and cross-complainant Glora Thomas.
Benjamin Graves; Law Offices of Benjamin C. Graves; 415-453-3600; appearing on behalf of plaintiff and cross-defendant Brandilynn S. Thomas.
Melissa A. Hoffman; Musacchio & Montanari, P.C.; 925-465-3950; appearing on behalf of defendants Norma Thomas and Jacquelynn Smith.

Mr. Underwood contests the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GLORIA THOMAS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO SET ASIDE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT is continued to January 19, 2023. The Court is inclined to set this settlement aside. However, the Court will give Plaintiff an opportunity to file a motion to enforce the settlement and to enter a judgment per the settlement agreement as it appears from the opposition that Plaintiff believes that the settlement agreement is enforceable. Plaintiff shall file and properly notice Motion to Enforce to be heard on January 19, 2023. The Court notes that the moving party failed to cooperate with the probate proceedings and failed to address this failure in the reply. This persistent failure contradicts moving party's claim that she "remains interested in an informal resolution of this suit and will earnestly attempt one as soon as all proper persons are parties to the suit."

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
